LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BAY AREA MEN'S SENIOR BASEBALL LEAGUE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT BAY AREA MEN'S SENIOR BASEBALL LEAGUE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE LEAGUE DID NOT SATISFY ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN OF PRESENTING ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT IT DID NOT ACT GROSSLY NEGLIGENTLY REGARDING THE INCIDENT THAT ASSERTEDLY HARMED PLAINTIFF STUART VIDNAGE. THE LEAGUE'S REPLY MEMORANDUM ACKNOWLEDGES THAT THE RELEASE OF LIABILITY PROVISION IS NOT A DEFENSE TO GROSS NEGLIGENCE, YET NOWHERE IN THE LEAGUE'S SEPARATE STATEMENT ARE THERE ANY FACTS SHOWING THAT THE LEAGUE DID NOT ACT GROSSLY NEGLIGENT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-13-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion Of Motion For Default Prove Up Hearing Against Defendant Parkhurst
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant Jonathan Parkhurst.

Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 10:07:58 a.m. and concluding at 10:23:10 a.m.(D505)

Artady Itkin, Esq. representing Plaintiff Stuart Vindanage who placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.

Counsel provides a brief summary for the request for judgment. Counsel updates the court as to the criminal case against defendant.

Plaintiff testifies to the request for general damages.

Good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and awards judgment as follows:

The Plaintiff shall recover from the Defendant Jonathan Parkhurst, the following:

General Damages $2,000,000.00
Medical Costs $387,192.00
Attorney's Fees $0
Interest $0

Total Judgment $2,387,192.00

Proposed judgment on judicial council form shall be submitted for review and signature of the court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Arkady Itkin, Esq. 415-640-6765 for Plaintiff.
Taric Monsour, Esq. 510-254-6767 Kronenberg Law for Defendant (in-person).

ARGUED. Mr. Monsour argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BAY AREA MEN'S SENIOR BASEBALL LEAGUE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant Bay Area Men's Senior Baseball League's motion for summary judgment is denied. The League did not satisfy its initial summary judgment burden of presenting admissible evidence showing that it did not act grossly negligently regarding the incident that assertedly harmed plaintiff Stuart Vidnage. The League's reply memorandum acknowledges that the release of liability provision is not a defense to gross negligence, yet nowhere in the League's separate statement are there any facts showing that the League did not act grossly negligent.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling approved as to form.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion Of Motion For Default Prove Up Hearing Against Defendant Parkhurst
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Continued To Mar-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for Default Judgment against Defendant Jonathan Parkhurst.

Dept.505: Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record", commencing at 10:02:54 a.m., and concluding at 10:10:14 a.m. (D505)

Appearances made:
Arkady Itkin, Esq. representing Plaintiff Stuart Vindanage, who was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.

Court discusses procedural issues with counsel.

Plaintiff is to serve the Statement of Damages pursuant to CCP 425.11(d)(1) and to provide courtesy copies of the default packet to the court.

The Court continues this matter to allow for Plaintiff to cure issues and to provide courtesy copies at least 5 court days before the hearing.

The matter is reset for March 28, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept.505.
